Home»Lifestyle»Celeb News»International Celebs»Snoop Dogg is NBC’s Special Correspondent for the Paris Olympics

Snoop Dogg takes centre stage as NBC’s Special Correspondent for the Paris Olympics,  promising a  unique perspective.

In a surprising twist, the iconic rapper and media personality, Snoop Dogg, is set to join NBC’s coverage of the 2024 Summer Olympics in Paris. The network revealed that the 52-year-old multi-talented artist will be a Special Correspondent for the Olympic Primetime Show, offering his unique take on the events.

ALSO READ: Hannah Waddingham passionately defends Tom Cruise criticism

Scheduled to kick off on 26 July 2024, the Paris Olympics will witness Snoop Dogg on the ground, reporting directly from the heart of the action.

NBC promises viewers a one-of-a-kind experience as Snoop explores Parisian landmarks, attends competitions, and interacts with athletes, their families, and friends as reported byPeople.

ALSO READ: Rosario Dawson, 44, excited as she preps for grandmotherhood

Known for his laid-back charm and infectious enthusiasm, Snoop is expected to inject his signature “Snoop style” into the coverage. This will make these games truly unforgettable.

A veteran Olympics enthusiast, Snoop expressed his excitement, saying, “I grew up watching the Olympics, and am thrilled to see the incredible athletes bring their A-game to Paris.

ALSO READ: Jodie Turner-Smith strolls with friend amidst ex’s new romance

It’s a celebration of skill, dedication, and the pursuit of greatness.” With a promise to elevate, celebrate, and make the games unforgettable.  He concluded by saying, “Let’s smoke the competition and may the best shine like gold. Peace and Olympic LOVE, ya dig?”

This won’t be Snoop’s first rodeo with Olympics coverage. He previously teamed up with Kevin Hart for a highlight show during the 2020 Tokyo Games.

ALSO READ: Florence Pugh takes a hit: Dodging objects at CCXP

Molly Solomon, NBC Olympic Production president and executive producer, credited Snoop’s performance during the Tokyo Olympics for earning him the role of Special Correspondent in Paris.

Molly stated, “We don’t know what the heck is going to happen every day, but we know he will add his unique perspective to our re-imagined Olympic primetime show.”

ALSO READ: Royal decree: King Charles’ extravagant demands unveiled!

The announcement, made during NBC’s Sunday Night Football game, revealed that Snoop has already filmed segments with Team USA athletes, including gymnast Suni Lee, basketball star A’ja Wilson, skateboarder Jagger Eaton, and beach volleyball duo Kelly Cheng and Sara Hughes.

As Paris prepares to host the Olympics for the third time, the city is reimagining the Games with competitions set against iconic landmarks like the Eiffel Tower and the Palace of Versailles.

ALSO READ: Taylor Swift shines bright at Beyoncé’s London premiere

With Snoop Dogg in the mix, viewers can undoubtedly expect a blend of entertainment, humour, and an authentic Snoop Dogg twist to the 2024 Paris Olympics coverage. Get ready to witness the games like never before, as Snoop Dogg brings his unparalleled style to the world’s greatest sporting event.
